# neotoma.io: SERP snippet spacing and sitelinks

## Background
- Google sometimes concatenated homepage hero text as `forget.Neotoma` because the `<h1>` split the headline across two `display:block` spans.
- Sitelinks are algorithmic; there is no switch to enable them.

## Work items

### 1. Hero markup (crawler-safe snippet)
- [x] Merge hero `<h1>` into one block with an explicit `<br />` between the first line and the emphasized product name (`frontend/src/components/SitePage.tsx`).
- [ ] Ship to production and request re-index for `https://neotoma.io/` in Google Search Console (URL Inspection).

### 2. Sitelinks positioning (indirect)
- [ ] Keep a shallow, consistent IA: important destinations (`/install`, `/docs`, `/evaluate`, pricing or equivalents) linked from global nav and footer with real `<a href>` in prerendered HTML where applicable.
- [ ] Ensure each major URL has a unique `<title>` and meta description (source: `frontend/src/site/seo_metadata.ts` and generated `site_pages/`).
- [ ] Submit / refresh `sitemap.xml` in Search Console; fix crawl errors if any.
- [ ] After deploy, monitor branded queries (`neotoma`, `neotoma ai`) for sitelink appearance over weeks (not guaranteed).

### 3. Static export hygiene
- [ ] Regenerate `site_pages/` (or your static export pipeline) so deployed HTML reflects the hero change and current meta tags.

### 4. Optional consistency pass
- [ ] Align default homepage meta description between `frontend/index.html` and live `site_pages/index.html` / `seo_metadata.ts` if intentional drift exists.

## Out of scope
- Buying or manipulating sitelinks; Google-only behavior beyond best-practice IA and signals.
